model.label: Gemini 3.1 Pro Preview
model.description: Google DeepMind • Preview Gemini model tuned for high-end multimodal reasoning and long-context analysis.

pick.rationale: In this highly anticipated Round of 32 matchup, St. John's and Kansas enter with nearly identical statistical profiles, both ranking just inside the top 25 in KenPom and Torvik. The game projects as a defensive battle, with the Jayhawks boasting the 10th-ranked defense nationally and the Red Storm right behind them at 12th. However, St. John's holds a slight edge on the offensive end and carries the momentum of a stellar 27-6 season in the Big East. In a contest where points will be at a premium, the Red Storm's marginally superior offensive execution gives them the narrow advantage needed to outlast Kansas and advance to the Sweet 16.

reasoningStep.title: Analyze St. John's vs Kansas Matchup
reasoningStep.summary: St. John's holds a slight statistical edge over Kansas in overall efficiency, driven by a marginally better offense.
evidence: St. John's ranks 18th in Torvik and 20th in KenPom, compared to Kansas's 22nd and 21st rankings.
Both teams feature elite defenses (Kansas 10th, St. John's 12th), but St. John's has a slightly better offense (46th vs 57th) and a superior 27-6 record.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
